topic.what: Accelerating ZKP kernels (MSM and NTT) on AI ASICs (specifically Google TPU) using hardware-aware complexity analysis and specialized optimizations
topic.how: Introduces Big-T complexity model, MXU-centric RNS lazy reduction to convert high-precision modular arithmetic to dense matrix operations, and layout-stationary dataflows (LS-PPG and 5-step NTT) to eliminate expensive shuffles and inter-device communication
topic.why_matters: ZKP provers remain prohibitively expensive; AI ASICs offer superior energy efficiency at scale; existing Big-O analysis fails to capture heterogeneous compute and layout transformation costs on modern AI accelerators

characteristics.what_it_measures: NTT throughput improvement over GPU baseline (GZKP V100)
characteristics.evidence: Table 3: 753-bit NTT at degree 214 shows 150us vs 15.1us; 753-bit NTT at degree 224 shows OOM on V100 vs 21.4s on TPUv6e8
characteristics.context: Scales with precision; larger precision shows greater speedup due to RNS efficiency vs Montgomery carry chains

characteristics.what_it_measures: MSM throughput improvement over GPU baseline
characteristics.evidence: Table 3: 753-bit MSM at N=222 shows 2.66ms vs 2.24ms; at N=226 shows 40.70ms vs 35.64ms
characteristics.context: Less impressive than NTT due to random scatter/gather bottleneck in Bucketize phase

characteristics.what_it_measures: Latency reduction from MXU-centric RNS lazy reduction vs radix Montgomery
characteristics.evidence: Section 4.2.1: MXU-centric RNS Lazy Reduction removes sequential carry propagation; Fig 6 ablation shows arithmetic optimization provides dominant speedup
characteristics.context: 90x speedup specifically for modular reduction; overall arithmetic optimization yields 50-90x for MSM

characteristics.what_it_measures: Modular multiplication latency improvement across batch sizes
characteristics.evidence: Section 4.4 and Fig 7: Gap widens with increasing precision (256 to 753 bits) and batch size
characteristics.context: Performance gap amplifies with higher precision and larger batch sizes due to MXU utilization

characteristics.what_it_measures: Speedup from dataflow optimizations (LS-PPG) for MSM
characteristics.evidence: Section 4.2.2: LS-PPG eliminates inter-TPU communication and intra-TPU layout reorganization; BA dominates runtime due to random scatter
characteristics.context: Dataflow optimization reduces BR (bucket reduction) by ~6x from increased parallelism

characteristics.what_it_measures: 5-step vs 3-step NTT performance at high degrees
characteristics.evidence: Section 4.2.3: 5-step NTT faster at degrees > 222 by reducing twiddle parameter storage; 3-step better at smaller degrees
characteristics.context: Benefit appears at higher degrees where factors become large enough to fully utilize VRegs (8x128)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Big-T guided optimizations speedup over baseline
characteristics.evidence: Contributions section: Big-T complexity analysis guides LS-PPG (5.8x) and 5-step NTT (1.6x) construction
characteristics.context: Big-T exposes bottlenecks hidden by Big-O analysis
